On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::extract::State;\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\npub struct JwtIssuer {\n    secret: Vec<u8>,\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\n    pub issuer_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl JwtIssuer {\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\n    pub fn verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\n\n        decode::<JwtClaims>(token, &DecodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret), &validation)\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature => AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                _ => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\n///\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\n#[derive(Default)]\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    inner: RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>,\n}\n\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    /// Create an empty cache.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\n    pub fn contains(&self, jti: &Uuid) -> bool {\n        self.inner\n            .read()\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\n    pub fn insert(&self, jti: Uuid) {\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\n            g.insert(jti);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\n///\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\n    pub issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc<InMemoryRevocationCache>,\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\n    pub storage: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Convenience constructor.\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\n///\n/// Install with:\n/// ```ignore\n/// Router::new()\n///     .route(\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\", …)\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n///         Arc::new(state),\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\n///     ))\n/// ```\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\n    State(state): State<Arc<AuthPipelineState>>,\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(&path) else {\n        return next.run(request).await;\n    };\n\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\n    let database = database.to_string();\n    let method = request.method().clone();\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\n\n    match verify_request(&state, &headers, &method, &tenant, &database) {\n        Ok(identity) => {\n            let mut request = request;\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\n            next.run(request).await\n        }\n        Err(err) => AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn verify_request(\n    state: &AuthPipelineState,\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    method: &Method,\n    tenant: &str,\n    database: &str,\n) -> Result<ResolvedIdentity, AuthError> {\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\n\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\n\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\n    }\n\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\n    // Expired: exp + skew < now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) < now {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\n    }\n    // Not yet valid: nbf > now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\n    if claims.nbf > now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\n        .jti\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(&jti_uuid) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n    }\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        if storage\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\n        {\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\n            drop(storage);\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\n    }\n\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\n    let user_id = UserId::new(&claims.sub);\n    let user = {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n    };\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\n    }\n\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\n    let granted_db = claims\n        .grants\n        .find_database(database)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\n\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\n        Op::Read\n    } else {\n        Op::Write\n    };\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\n    }\n\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\n        user_id,\n        tenant_id,\n        grants: claims.grants,\n    })\n}\n\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Extract the `Bearer <token>` value from the `Authorization` header.\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Result<&str, AuthError> {\n    let auth_header = headers\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n\n    let auth_str = auth_header\n        .to_str()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n\n    auth_str\n        .strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n}\n\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\n///\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)> {\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\n        (Some(\"tenants\"), Some(tenant), Some(\"databases\"), Some(rest))\n            if !tenant.is_empty() && !rest.is_empty() =>\n        {\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\n            if database.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some((tenant, database))\n            }\n        }\n        _ => None,\n    }\n}\n\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\n\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\n        let body = json!({\n            \"error\": {\n                \"code\": self.0.error_code(),\n                \"message\": self.0.to_string()\n            }\n        });\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew → 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 31; // 31 seconds in the past, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 6: `jti` revoked → 401 credential_revoked\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jti_revoked() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown → 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_foreign_issuer\");\n}\n\n/// Row 8: `aud` ≠ URL tenant → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_wrong_tenant() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.aud = \"other-tenant\".to_string(); // URL says \"acme\"\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended → 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Build state with a suspended user.\n    let mut storage = make_storage();\n    // Overwrite the user with a suspended version.\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Suspended\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: Some(1000),\n    });\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"user_suspended\");\n}\n\n/// Row 10: `sub` not a tenant member → 403 not_a_tenant_member\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_tenant_member() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Storage with user but NO tenant membership.\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"No Member\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"not_a_tenant_member\");\n}\n\n/// Row 11: URL database not in grants → 403 database_not_granted\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_database_not_granted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    // Grant is for \"products\", URL database is \"orders\"\n    claims.grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"products\".to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"database_not_granted\");\n}\n\n/// Row 12: Op not in grant → 403 op_not_granted\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_op_not_granted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    // Grant only has Read; we'll POST (Write).\n    claims.grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n\n    // POST = Write, but grant only has Read.\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"POST\")\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"op_not_granted\");\n}\n\n// ── valid_jwt_populates_request_extension ─────────────────────────────────────\n\nasync fn identity_handler(\n    Extension(identity): Extension<ResolvedIdentity>,\n) -> impl IntoResponse {\n    axum::Json(serde_json::json!({\"user_id\": identity.user_id.as_str()}))\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn valid_jwt_populates_request_extension() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(identity_handler))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ));\n\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n    assert_eq!(json[\"user_id\"].as_str().unwrap(), USER_ID);\n}\n\n// ── revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second ─────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app_before = make_app(state.clone());\n    let app_after = make_app(state.clone());\n\n    // First request must succeed.\n    let (status, _) = send(app_before, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK, \"before revocation should be 200\");\n\n    // Directly invalidate the cache — no sleep needed.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    // Second request must fail with credential_revoked.\n    let (status, json) = send(app_after, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n// ── clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected ─────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_exp_29s_accepted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.exp = now - 29; // 29 s ago — within the 30s skew window\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, _) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_exp_31s_rejected() {\n    let issuer = token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, json) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_nbf_29s_future_accepted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 29; // 29 s from now — within the 30s skew window\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, _) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn clock_skew_nbf_31s_future_rejected() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 s from now — beyond the 30s skew window\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let (status, json) = send(make_app(state), bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n// ── ops_matrix ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn ops_matrix() {\n    use http::Method;\n\n    struct Case {\n        method: Method,\n        ops: Vec<Op>,\n        expected_status: u16,\n    }\n    let cases = vec![\n        // GET (Read) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        // Write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n    ];\n\n    // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n    let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n    for case in cases {\n        let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n        let mut claims = valid_claims();\n        claims.grants = Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: case.ops,\n            }],\n        };\n        let token = issuer_inner.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n        let state = make_state();\n        // Router that handles any method on TEST_PATH.\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\n                TEST_PATH,\n                axum::routing::any(handler),\n            )\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n                state,\n                jwt_verify_layer,\n            ));\n\n        let req = Request::builder()\n            .method(case.method.clone())\n            .uri(TEST_PATH)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n            .body(Body::empty())\n            .unwrap();\n        let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            resp.status().as_u16(),\n            case.expected_status,\n            \"method={} expected {}\",\n            case.method,\n            case.expected_status\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ── error_code / status_code consistency ──────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn auth_error_status_and_code_coverage() {\n    // Verify every variant maps to the correct status and code string.\n    let cases: &[(AuthError, u16, &str)] = &[\n        (AuthError::Unauthenticated, 401, \"unauthenticated\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialMalformed, 401, \"credential_malformed\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialInvalid, 401, \"credential_invalid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialExpired, 401, \"credential_expired\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid, 401, \"credential_not_yet_valid\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialRevoked, 401, \"credential_revoked\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer, 401, \"credential_foreign_issuer\"),\n        (AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant, 403, \"credential_wrong_tenant\"),\n        (AuthError::UserSuspended, 401, \"user_suspended\"),\n        (AuthError::NotATenantMember, 403, \"not_a_tenant_member\"),\n        (AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted, 403, \"database_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::OpNotGranted, 403, \"op_not_granted\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsExceedIssuerRole, 401, \"grants_exceed_issuer_role\"),\n        (AuthError::GrantsMalformed, 401, \"grants_malformed\"),\n    ];\n    for (err, expected_status, expected_code) in cases {\n        assert_eq!(\n            err.status_code(),\n            *expected_status,\n            \"status mismatch for {:?}\",\n            err\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            err.error_code(),\n            *expected_code,\n            \"code mismatch for {:?}\",\n            err\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ── revocation via storage fallback ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn revocation_via_storage_fallback() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    // Build storage with the JTI revoked at the storage level.\n    let mut storage = make_storage();\n    storage.revoke_jti(jti_uuid);\n\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRLMXUs4TZUS5Hhv4m4p8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QqPQtyiL6uPht7wAo243yE","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs","content":"//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":575,"cache_read_input_tokens":132304,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":575},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"cc1c03fa-01b8-4915-97d1-edc4d799b3f6"}

))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n10\t\n11\tuse axum::body::Body;\n12\tuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\n13\tuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\n14\tuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n15\t\n16\t/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n17\t///\n18\t/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n19\t/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n20\t#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n21\t#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\n22\tstruct UiAssets;\n23\t\n24\t/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n25\t///\n26\t/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n27\t/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n28\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\n29\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n30\t    let raw = uri.path();\n31\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n32\t    let asset_path = raw\n33\t        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n34\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n35\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\n36\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n37\t        \"index.html\"\n38\t    } else {\n39\t        asset_path\n40\t    };\n41\t\n42\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\tfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n46\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n47\t        Some(content) => {\n48\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n49\t            Response::builder()\n50\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n51\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n52\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n53\t        }\n54\t        None => {\n55\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n56\t            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n57\t                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n58\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n59\t                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n60\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n61\t                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n62\t            }\n63\t        }\n64\t    }\n65\t}\n66\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"639a3f3a-e27d-4d23-b9e3-a0b9bc507e2b","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:45:23.541Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/src/embedded_ui.rs","content":"//! Embedded admin UI — assets from `ui/build` compiled into the binary.\n//!\n//! The `UiAssets` struct embeds every file under `ui/build` at compile time.\n//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n//!\n//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n//! Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

boundary at ±30 s\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ── ADR-018 §4 failure-mode table ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header → 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array → 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid → 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew → 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

changed\n\n### axon-core (additive)\n- `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` — installed by middleware\n- `AuthError::status_code()` / `error_code()` — 401/403 + stable code strings\n- // registerSubcommands adds all subcommands to the root command
func (f *CommandFactory) registerSubcommands(rootCmd *cobra.Command) {
	// Version command
	versionCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "version",
		Short: "Show version information",
		Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
			_, _ = fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), f.versionOutput())
			f.warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd)

			// Check for --no-check flag
			noCheck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-check")
			_ = noCheck // TODO: Implement update checking when this flag is used
			// For now, we don't check for updates
		},
	}
	versionCmd.Flags().Bool("no-check", false, "Skip checking for updates")
	rootCmd.AddCommand(versionCmd)

	// Completion command
	completionCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell]",
		Short: "Generate completion script",
		Long: `To configure your shell to load completions:

Bash:
  echo 'source <(ddx completion bash)' >> ~/.bashrc

Zsh:
  echo 'source <(ddx completion zsh)' >> ~/.zshrc

Fish:
  ddx completion fish | source

PowerShell:
  ddx completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression
`,
		DisableFlagsInUseLine: true,
		ValidArgs:             []string{"bash", "zsh", "fish", "powershell"},
		Args:                  cobra.MatchAll(cobra.ExactArgs(1), cobra.OnlyValidArgs),
		Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
			switch args[0] {
			case "bash":
				_ = rootCmd.GenBashCompletion(os.Stdout)
			case "zsh":
				_ = rootCmd.GenZshCompletion(os.Stdout)
			case "fish":
				_ = rootCmd.GenFishCompletion(os.Stdout, true)
			case "powershell":
				_ = rootCmd.GenPowerShellCompletionWithDesc(os.Stdout)
			}
		},
	}
	rootCmd.AddCommand(completionCmd)

	// Register all other commands
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInitCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newListCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newDoctorCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newUpdateCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newUpgradeCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newConfigCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newPersonaCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newStatusCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newLogCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newBeadCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newArtifactCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newExecCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newMetricCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newMetricsCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newAcCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newDocCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newCheckpointCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newServerCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newSkillsCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstalledCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newUninstallCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newSearchCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newOutdatedCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newVerifyCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newJqCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newTryCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newSyncCommand())

	// Add prompts command group
	promptsCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:     "prompts",
		Short:   "Manage AI prompts",
		Aliases: []string{"prompt"},
	}
	promptsCmd.AddCommand(f.newPromptsListCommand())
	promptsCmd.AddCommand(f.newPromptsShowCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(promptsCmd)
}

		return
	}

	if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
		return

data.text: I’m at the edit point now. The implementation will be: add a root `ddx cleanup` command, make the shared cleanup manager support dry-run vs apply, then add command and manager tests plus a short operator-doc update.
The code path is in place. I’m adding one manager-level dry-run test now so the new `--apply` behavior is covered where it actually lives, not only through the CLI wrapper.

refsPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".git", "refs", "ddx", "iterations", "ddx-preserved")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(refsPath, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(refsPath, "attempt-1"), []byte("abcdef1234567890"), 0o644))

	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, &executionCleanupTestGitOps{})
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot
	mgr.Probe = executionCleanupTestProbe{
		live: map[string]bool{
			filepath.Clean(activePath): true,
		},
	}

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.DirExists(t, activePath)
	assert.DirExists(t, preservedPath)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(executionsDir, "manifest.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(executionsDir, "result.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(refsPath, "attempt-1"))
	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), summary.RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, summary.CompleteEvidenceDirs)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, summary.Observations)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "preserved_temp_dir"))
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "complete_evidence"))
}

func TestExecutionCleanup_ReportsSummary(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot := setupExecutionCleanupProjectRoot(t)
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()

	stalePath := filepath.Join(tempRoot, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-summary-20260506T154739-99990000")
	writeExecutionCleanupCandidate(t, stalePath, ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		BeadID:       "ddx-summary",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-99990000",
		WorktreePath: stalePath,
	}, map[string]string{"payload.txt": strings.Repeat("x", 64)})

	executionsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions", "attempt-summary")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(executionsDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(executionsDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-summary"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(executionsDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))

	gitOps := &executionCleanupTestGitOps{listErr: errors.New("worktree list unavailable")}
	mgr := NewExecutionCleanupManager(projectRoot, gitOps)
	mgr.TempRoot = tempRoot

	summary, err := mgr.Cleanup(context.Background())
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), summary.RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs)
	assert.NotZero(t, summary.BytesReclaimed)
	assert.NotZero(t, summary.InodesReclaimed)
	require.NotEmpty(t, summary.Warnings)
	assert.Equal(t, "git_worktree_list", summary.Warnings[0].Class)
	require.NotEmpty(t, summary.Observations)
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "removed_unregistered_temp_dir"))
	assert.True(t, hasObservationClass(summary.Observations, "complete_evidence"))
}

func hasObservationClass(observations []ExecutionCleanupObservation, class string) bool {
	for _, obs := range observations {
		if obs.Class == class {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type cleanupCommandJSON struct {
	DryRun                      bool             `json:"dry_run"`
	ProjectRoot                 string           `json:"project_root"`
	TempRoot                    string           `json:"temp_root"`
	ScannedTempDirs             int              `json:"scanned_temp_dirs"`
	ScannedEvidenceDirs         int              `json:"scanned_evidence_dirs"`
	CompleteEvidenceDirs        int              `json:"complete_evidence_dirs"`
	RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs int64            `json:"removed_unregistered_temp_dirs"`
	RemovedRegisteredWorktrees  int64            `json:"removed_registered_worktrees"`
	RemovedRunStateFiles        int64            `json:"removed_run_state_files"`
	BytesReclaimed              int64            `json:"bytes_reclaimed"`
	InodesReclaimed             int64            `json:"inodes_reclaimed"`
	Warnings                    []map[string]any `json:"warnings"`
	BlockedErrors               []map[string]any `json:"blocked_errors"`
	Observations                []map[string]any `json:"observations"`
}

func setupCleanupCommandProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
	t.Helper()
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	t.Setenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR", tempRoot)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"), 0o755))
	return projectRoot, tempRoot
}

func writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t *testing.T, root, name, projectRoot, attemptID string) string {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(root, name)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(path, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(path, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    attemptID,
		WorktreePath: path,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(path, "scratch.txt"), []byte("stale\n"), 0o644))
	return path
}

func TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-dryrun-20260506T154739-deadbeef", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-deadbeef")
	runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", agent.RunStateFileName)
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-deadbeef",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: would remove 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "run again with --apply")
	assert.DirExists(t, stalePath)
	assert.FileExists(t, runStatePath)
}

func TestCleanupCommand_ApplyRemovesStaleDDxResources(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-apply-20260506T154739-feedface", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-feedface")
	runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", agent.RunStateFileName)
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-feedface",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))
	evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions", "attempt-complete")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-complete"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: removed 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")
	assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)
	assert.NoFileExists(t, runStatePath)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"))
}

func TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-json-20260506T154739-12345678", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-12345678")
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-12345678",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var report cleanupCommandJSON
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &report))

	assert.True(t, report.DryRun)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.RemovedUnregisteredTempDirs)
	assert.Equal(t, int64(1), report.RemovedRunStateFiles)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, report.Warnings)
	assert.NotNil(t, report.BlockedErrors)
	assert.NotNil(t, report.Observations)
	assert.Greater(t, report.BytesReclaimed, int64(0))
	assert.Greater(t, report.InodesReclaimed, int64(0))
}

data.text: The failures are from the liveness probe, not the command plumbing: the test run-state used the same `AttemptID` as the candidate, so the manager treated it as live and preserved it. I’m fixing the fixtures to make the stale path actually stale.

data.text: The stale run-state issue is in the manager itself: the current code self-matches the run-state and never clears it. I’m fixing that by only preserving the run-state when a scanned live temp resource actually matches it.
